title: buxus sempervirens var. japonica
intro: Buxus sempervirens var. japonica, also known as Japanese boxwood, is a variety of the boxwood plant that is native to Japan and is known for its compact, evergreen growth and attractive foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Buxus sempervirens var. japonica include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, Buxus sempervirens var. japonica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
